response:
jamul indian village is known for its rich cultural heritage and strong sense of community the village is planning to build a new community center with a unique design the center will be constructed in the shape of a regular hexagon with each side measuring 30 meters the community also decided to incorporate a circular garden in the center of the hexagon with the garden's circumference touching the midpoints of the hexagon's sides  

the area of the hexagonal community center can be calculated by using the formula for the area of a regular hexagon which is 6 times the area of one of its equilateral triangles the side length is 30 meters so the area of one triangle is (sqrt(3)/4) * (30)^2 the total area of the hexagon is 6 * (sqrt(3)/4) * 900 = 1350 * sqrt(3) square meters the radius of the circular garden can be determined by finding the distance from the center of the hexagon to the midpoint of one of its sides this distance is equal to the apothem of the hexagon the apothem can be calculated using the formula (s * sqrt(3))/2 where s is the side length the apothem is (30 * sqrt(3))/2 = 15 * sqrt(3) meters the area of the circular garden is pi * (15 * sqrt(3))^2 = pi * 675 * 3 = 2025 * pi square meters
